zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Campbell於1456年2月25日出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾音巴拉雷時，她的父親Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll22歲，母親Isabel Elizabeth Stewart19歲。她於1474年9月14日與Sir George Seton在蘇格蘭可拉可曼南史爾多拉亞結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1530年7月11日在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾普雷斯頓潘斯享年74歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾阿爾哈坦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Campbell于1456年2月25日出生在苏格兰阿吉爾音巴拉雷时，她的父亲Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll22岁，母亲Isabel Elizabeth Stewart19岁。她于1474年9月14日与Sir George Seton在苏格兰可拉可曼南史爾多拉亞结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1530年7月11日在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾普雷斯頓潘斯享年74岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿吉爾阿爾哈坦。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Campbell was born on 25 February 1456, in Inveraray, Argyll, Scotland, her father, Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll, was 22 and her mother, Isabel Elizabeth Stewart, was 19. She married Sir George Seton on 14 September 1474, in Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 11 July 1530, in Prestonpans, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, at the age of 74, and was buried in Ardchattan, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Campbell est née le 25 février 1456, à Inveraray, Argyll, Écosse, son père, Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Isabel Elizabeth Stewart, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Sir George Seton le 14 septembre 1474, à Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 11 juillet 1530, à Prestonpans, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Ardchattan, Argyll, Écosse.

Februar 1456 in Inveraray, Argyll, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll 22 und ihre Mutter Isabel Elizabeth Stewart 19. Sie hat am 14. September 1474 in Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland Sir George Seton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 11. Juli 1530 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Prestonpans, Haddingtonshire, Scotland und wurde in Ardchattan, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

Morì il 11 luglio 1530, nel Prestonpans, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolta a Ardchattan, Argyll, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Campbellが1456年2月25日にスコットランドアルグイルインバーアレイで生まれた時，父Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyllは22歳，母Isabel Elizabeth Stewartは19歳であった。1474年9月14日にスコットランドクラクマナンシュアイアドラーでSir George Setonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1530年7月11日にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアプレストンプアンスで74歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルアルドチャッタンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Campbell(이)가 1456년 2월 25일 스컷랜드 아그이 인베라라이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Elizabeth Stewart(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1474년 9월 14일 스컷랜드 클랙먼아실르 돌라에서 Sir George Seton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1530년 7월 11일 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 플레스토파스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 아드차탠에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Campbell nasceu em 25 de fevereiro de 1456, em Inveraray, Argyll, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Elizabeth Stewart, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir George Seton em 14 de setembro de 1474, em Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Campbell родилась 25 февраля 1456 г. в местности инвирареи, аргилл, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Elizabeth Stewart, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir George Seton 14 сентября 1474 г. в местности доллар, клаккманнаньшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 11 июля 1530 г. в местности престонпанс, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности ардчаттань, аргилл, Шотландия.

es.25 February 1456–11 July 1530: 25 de febrero de 1456–11 de julio de 1530
es.Argyllshire, Scotland: Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Campbell nació el 25 de febrero de 1456, en Inveraray, Argyll, Scotland, su papá, Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Isabel Elizabeth Stewart, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Sir George Seton el 14 de septiembre de 1474, en Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 11 de julio de 1530, en Prestonpans, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultada en Ardchattan, Argyll, Scotland.
